articles.title: UK exposes Russian cyber unit hacking home routers to hijack internet traffic
articles.summary: Officials said the activity centers on compromising small office and home office routers and similar network devices exposed to the internet, often because of weak security settings or outdated software.

articles.title: GrafanaGhost: Attackers Can Abuse Grafana to Leak Enterprise Data
articles.summary: By targeting Grafana’s AI components, attackers can point to external resources and inject indirect prompts to bypass safeguards. The post GrafanaGhost: Attackers Can Abuse Grafana to Leak Enterprise Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Webinar Today: Why Automated Pentesting Alone Is Not Enough
articles.summary: Join the live diagnostic session to expose hidden coverage gaps and shift from flawed tool-level evaluations to a comprehensive, program-level validation discipline. The post Webinar Today: Why Automated Pentesting Alone Is Not Enough appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Over 1,000 Exposed ComfyUI Instances Targeted in Cryptomining Botnet Campaign
articles.summary: An active campaign has been observed targeting internet-exposed instances running ComfyUI, a popular stable diffusion platform, to enlist them into a cryptocurrency mining and proxy botnet. articles.title: Cyberattack hits Northern Ireland’s centralized school network, disrupting access for thousands
articles.summary: The Education Authority (EA), which oversees school support services in Northern Ireland, said in an official statement it became aware of the incident affecting the “C2K” system last week and took immediate steps to contain the breach.

articles.title: Iranian-Affiliated Cyber Actors Exploit Programmable Logic Controllers Across US Critical Infrastructure

articles.title: GPUBreach: Root Shell Access Achieved via GPU Rowhammer Attack
articles.summary: Researchers have demonstrated that GPU Rowhammer attacks can be used to escalate privileges. The post GPUBreach: Root Shell Access Achieved via GPU Rowhammer Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: The Hidden Cost of Recurring Credential Incidents
articles.summary: When talking about credential security, the focus usually lands on breach prevention. This makes sense when IBM’s 2025 Cost of a Data Breach Report puts the average cost of a breach at $4.4 million. Avoiding even one major incident is enough to justify most security investments,

articles.title: Medusa Ransomware Fast to Exploit Vulnerabilities, Breached Systems
articles.summary: The group is using zero-days, quickly weaponizes fresh bugs, and exfiltrates and encrypts data within days of initial access. The post Medusa Ransomware Fast to Exploit Vulnerabilities, Breached Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: German Police Unmask REvil Ransomware Leader
articles.summary: Shchukin is accused of extorting more than $2 million as the head of the GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations. The post German Police Unmask REvil Ransomware Leader appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: New GPUBreach Attack Enables Full CPU Privilege Escalation via GDDR6 Bit-Flips
articles.summary: New academic research has identified multiple RowHammer attacks against high-performance graphics processing units (GPUs) that could be exploited to escalate privileges and, in some cases, even take full control of a host. The efforts have been codenamed GPUBreach, GDDRHammer, and GeF

articles.title: China-Linked Storm-1175 Exploits Zero-Days to Rapidly Deploy Medusa Ransomware
articles.summary: A China-based threat actor known for deploying Medusa ransomware has been linked to the weaponization of a combination of zero-day and N-day vulnerabilities to orchestrate "high-velocity" attacks and break into susceptible internet-facing systems. "The threat actor's high operational tempo

articles.title: Wynn Resorts Says 21,000 Employees Affected by ShinyHunters Hack
articles.summary: The high-end casino and hotel operator has likely paid a ransom to avoid a data leak. The post Wynn Resorts Says 21,000 Employees Affected by ShinyHunters Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Flowise AI Agent Builder Under Active CVSS 10.0 RCE Exploitation; 12,000+ Instances Exposed
articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw in Flowise, an open-source artificial intelligence (AI) platform, according to new findings from VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2025-59528 (CVSS score: 10.0), a code injection vulnerability that could result in remot

articles.title: German authorities identify REvil and GandCrab ransomware bosses
articles.summary: The Federal Police in Germany (BKA) has identified two Russian nationals as the leaders of GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations between 2019 and 2021. [...]

articles.title: Cyberattack on telecom giant Rostelecom disrupts internet services across Russia
articles.summary: A “large-scale” distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack targeted the network of Russian state-run telecom giant Rostelecom on Monday evening, temporarily disrupting online banking, government platforms and other digital services across dozens of cities.

articles.title: New GPUBreach attack enables system takeover via GPU rowhammer
articles.summary: A new attack, dubbed GPUBreach, can induce Rowhammer bit-flips on GPU GDDR6 memories to escalate privileges and lead to a full system compromise. [...]

articles.title: Medusa ransomware group using zero-days to launch attacks within 24 hours of breach, Microsoft says
articles.summary: Microsoft said it has been alarmed to see how effective Medusa actors are, citing multiple cases where the group can move from initial access to data exfiltration and ransomware deployment within 24 hours.

articles.title: Disgruntled researcher leaks “BlueHammer” Windows zero-day exploit
articles.summary: Exploit code has been released for an unpatched Windows privilege escalation flaw reported privately to Microsoft, allowing attackers to gain SYSTEM or elevated administrator permissions. [...]

articles.title: Iran-Linked Password-Spraying Campaign Targets 300+ Israeli Microsoft 365 Organizations
articles.summary: An Iran-nexus threat actor is suspected to be behind a password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments in Israel and the U.A.E. amid ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The activity, assessed to be ongoing, was carried out in three distinct attack waves that took

articles.title: German police unmask two suspects linked to REvil ransomware gang
articles.summary: The suspects were named as Daniil Shchukin, a 31-year-old Russian national believed to have used the alias UNKN (UNKNOWN), and Anatoly Kravchuk, a 43-year-old Ukraine-born Russian citizen who investigators say worked as a developer for the group.

articles.title: Microsoft links Medusa ransomware affiliate to zero-day attacks
articles.summary: Microsoft says that Storm-1175, a China-based financially motivated cybercriminal group known for deploying Medusa ransomware payloads, has been deploying n-day and zero-day exploits in high-velocity attacks. [...]

articles.title: Singapore, US warn of latest Fortinet bug being exploited in wild
articles.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) gave federal agencies until Thursday to apply the hotfix.

articles.title: CISA orders feds to patch exploited Fortinet EMS flaw by Friday
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) ordered federal agencies to secure FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) instances against an actively exploited vulnerability by Friday. [...]

articles.title: Google DeepMind Researchers Map Web Attacks Against AI Agents
articles.summary: A vulnerability named ‘AI Agent Traps’ allows attackers to manipulate, deceive, and exploit visiting agents via malicious web content. The post Google DeepMind Researchers Map Web Attacks Against AI Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Why Simple Breach Monitoring is No Longer Enough
articles.summary: Infostealers are harvesting credentials and session cookies at scale, bypassing traditional defenses. Lunar explains why simple breach monitoring alone can't keep up with modern credential-based attacks. [...]

articles.title: Multi-OS Cyberattacks: How SOCs Close a Critical Risk in 3 Steps

articles.title: Hackers threaten to leak data after cyberattack on German party Die Linke
articles.summary: Die Linke confirmed in late March that its IT infrastructure had been hit by what it described as a “serious cyberattack.”

articles.summary: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. articles.summary: The most active piece of enterprise infrastructure in the company is the developer workstation. That laptop is where credentials are created, tested, cached, copied, and reused across services, bots, build tools, and now local AI agents. In March 2026, the TeamPCP threat act

articles.title: Guardarian Users Targeted With Malicious Strapi NPM Packages
articles.summary: Hackers published 36 NPM packages posing as Strapi plugins to execute shells, escape containers, and harvest credentials. The post Guardarian Users Targeted With Malicious Strapi NPM Packages appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: North Korean Hackers Target High-Profile Node.js Maintainers
articles.summary: The threat actor behind the Axios supply chain attack has been aiming at other maintainers in its social engineering campaign. The post North Korean Hackers Target High-Profile Node.js Maintainers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Qilin and Warlock Ransomware Use Vulnerable Drivers to Disable 300+ EDR Tools
articles.summary: Threat actors associated with Qilin and Warlock ransomware operations have been observed using the bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) technique to silence security tools running on compromised hosts, according to findings from Cisco Talos and Trend Micro. Qilin at

articles.title: Fortinet Rushes Emergency Fixes for Exploited Zero-Day
articles.summary: The improper access control bug in FortiClient EMS allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely. The post Fortinet Rushes Emergency Fixes for Exploited Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: Germany's Federal Criminal Police Office (aka BKA or the Bundeskriminalamt) has unmasked the real identities of two of the key figures associated with the now-defunct REvil (aka Sodinokibi) ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation. One of the threat actors, who went by the alias UNKN, functioned as

articles.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
articles.summary: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. articles.title: Hackers exploit React2Shell in automated credential theft campaign
articles.summary: Hackers are running a large-scale campaign to steal credentials in an automated way after exploiting React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) in vulnerable Next.js apps. [...]

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 36 malicious packages in the npm registry that are disguised as Strapi CMS plugins but come with different payloads to facilitate Redis and PostgreSQL exploitation, deploy reverse shells, harvest credentials, and drop a persistent implant. articles.title: Fortinet Patches Actively Exploited CVE-2026-35616 in FortiClient EMS
articles.summary: Fortinet has released out-of-band patches for a critical security flaw impacting FortiClient EMS that it said has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-35616 (CVSS score: 9.1), has been described as a pre-authentication API access bypass leading to privile

articles.title: Axios npm hack used fake Teams error fix to hijack maintainer account
articles.summary: The maintainers of the popular Axios HTTP client have published a detailed post-mortem describing how one of its developers was targeted by a social engineering campaign believed to have been conducted by North Korean threat actors. [...]

articles.title: Device code phishing attacks surge 37x as new kits spread online
articles.summary: Device code phishing attacks that abuse the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant flow to hijack accounts have surged more than 37 times this year. [...]

articles.title: European Commission Confirms Data Breach Linked to Trivy Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: Hackers stole over 300GB of data from the Commission’s AWS environment, including personal information. The post European Commission Confirms Data Breach Linked to Trivy Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA gives agencies two weeks to patch video conferencing bug exploited by Chinese hackers
articles.summary: A bug in a popular line of video conferencing software is being exploited by hackers, prompting the U.S. government to order all agencies to patch the vulnerability within two weeks.

articles.title: EU cyber agency attributes major data breach to TeamPCP hacking group
articles.summary: The European Union’s cybersecurity agency said the hacking group TeamPCP was behind a massive recent data breach at the European Commission.

articles.title: Hims & Hers warns of data breach after Zendesk support ticket breach
articles.summary: Telehealth giant Hims & Hers Health is warning that it suffered a data breach after support tickets were stolen from a third-party customer service platform. [...]
